channel.title: GitHub Copilot Bulletin — English / Biweekly / Devs
channel.description: Latest GitHub Copilot updates for Devs (Biweekly, English)

item.title: Copilot Bulletin Biweekly #3 — April 1 to April 15, 2026 · Devs Edition
item.description: The past two weeks reveal a clear pattern in Copilot's evolution: greater developer autonomy and mobility. VS Code gained a fully autonomous Autopilot mode, the CLI shed its dependency on GitHub-hosted models with BYOK support, and agent sessions can now be monitored and steered remotely from any device. Copilot is becoming less tied to a single desk or tool.

item.title: Copilot Bulletin Biweekly #2 — March 23 to April 6, 2026 · Devs Edition
item.description: The past two weeks mark a clear inflection point in Copilot's agentic story for developers. From parallel agent dispatching in the CLI, to a new SDK for embedding agentic workflows in any application, to verified commits and expanded IDE tooling—these updates form a cohesive foundation for developer-led AI automation. The tools to delegate, build, and trust agents are converging.

item.title: Copilot Bulletin Biweekly #1 — March 2 to March 16, 2026 · Devs Edition
item.description: The past two weeks mark a turning point in Copilot's agentic capabilities. Both VS Code and JetBrains IDEs shipped major agent upgrades — hooks, plugins, memory sharing, custom agents — while GPT-5.4 landed with stronger multi-step reasoning. Copilot is moving from code assistant to programmable development partner.
